collection.description: The Arcanum is a curated collection of my best homebrew characters, meant to work well across a variety of different scripts. Feel free to use these in your own scripts, but be sure to credit me (https://tinyurl.com/Squ4ll, Squ4ll on Discord, and/or the symbol ₳ to signify a character from the Arcanum) when you do. I only want to put the best of the best in here, so releases will probably be slow. Thank you for your patience!
collection.tagline: A curated archive of arcane homebrew characters.
collection.author: Squ4ll

characters.name: Curator
characters.ability: You start knowing 3 players who are not Minions.
characters.creator: Squ4ll
characters.lede: The Curator identifies which players aren't Minions.
summaryBullets: On the Curator's first night, the Curator learns three players. These players can be any character type, except for Minions.
The Curator can learn alive players, dead players, evil players, good players, Demons, Townsfolk, Outsiders, or Travellers. It just cannot learn Minions.
A drunk or poisoned Curator can learn a Minion player.
howToRun: While preparing the first night, mark three players who are not Minions with the Curator's [[KNOW]] reminders.
During the first night, wake the Curator. Point to all three players marked KNOW. Put the Curator to sleep.
examples: Nathan is the Curator. On the first night, he learns Will, Jessica, and Angela. Will is the Chef, Jessica is the Ogre, and Angela is the Imp.
tips: Come out with your information right away! If you tell the group that you are the Noble, and which three players you learned, the group can use this information.
Wait to share your information! If evil tries to frame a certain player as a Minion, you can interject and call them out, revealing that you know they aren't a Minion.
characters.quote: Over here is our newest collection: 'Demons Through the Ages'. They had their help, of course, but it is only their legacy that remains to this day. Their Minions simply haven't the potential to belong here.

characters.name: Howl
characters.ability: If you are "mad" you are a not-in-play character, you might become it.
characters.creator: Squ4ll
summaryBullets: “Madness” is a term that means “you are trying to convince the group of something.” So, if the Howl player is mad about being a character, this means they are trying to convince people that they are that character.

Simply hinting is not enough. The player must make a decent effort to convince the group. Mad players are never literally forced to say things they don’t want to—but if the Storyteller doesn’t hear them make an effort, they will not be rewarded.
The Howl can only become characters that are not in play. If they are mad about being an in-play character, nothing happens.

The Storyteller will usually make the Howl become the first not in play character they are mad about being. This way, if the Howl does not change characters, they can be reasonably sure that the character is in play.

The Howl can become any not-in-play character, even Minions or Demons, but doesn’t change alignments this way. They can change characters at any time. If they change characters during the day, they learn this immediately the following night.
howToRun: At any time (even in the day), if you believe the Howl is mad about being a character that is not in play, then you can choose that they become that character. Replace the Howl’s token with the new character token. As soon as possible at night, wake the player. Show them the [[YOU ARE]] info token, then their new character token, then put them to sleep. (You may need to turn character tokens upside-down to remind you that a player’s alignment is opposite to the color of the token. You may need to remind the player that their alignment is unchanged, by giving a thumbs-up or thumbs-down.)
examples: Joshua is the Howl. On the first day, he tells everyone that he’s the Investigator and claims that 1 of 2 players is a Minion. Sam is the Investigator, so nothing happens. The next day, Joshua shamefully tells everyone he is actually the Butler. That night, Joshua becomes the Butler.

Sabrina is the Howl. She is quiet for the first few days. On the third day, she tells everyone that she is an evil Legion, and claims that since there is a Poppy Grower, she doesn’t know who the other Legion are. Sabrina immediately becomes a good Legion. Later, when only evil players vote today, the execution fails.
tips: Be mad about being someone else’s character! Claim to be something that you know someone else is claiming to be. If you change characters, they’re lying.
Be mad about being a character that no one is claiming to be! Knowing that there’s a hidden Snake Charmer, Sage, or Heretic can be a huge benefit to your team!
Be mad about being an evil character! If you don’t become the Shabaloth even if everyone now thinks you are the Demon, you can be reasonably sure that there is a Shabaloth in play!
characters.quote: Look, I know I’ve been lying a lot recently, so I’ll come clean. I am Legion. I am evil and I am Legion, and I don’t know who the other Legion are. Please! You have to believe me!

characters.name: Corsair
characters.ability: You start knowing a clue to the Treasure. If the Demon finds it, evil wins, even if you die. [Treasure is hidden]
characters.creator: Squ4ll
characters.lede: The Corsair must find the buried treasure.
summaryBullets: Treasure is a character token physically hidden somewhere in the space where you are playing before the game starts.

On the first night, the Corsair learns a clue about the Treasure’s location. The clue can be anything the Storyteller thinks will help the Corsair find the Treasure, whether it be a phrase written on paper or typed out on a phone, a gesture, a word, or something else entirely.
If the Demon holds the Treasure and shows it to the Storyteller, the evil team wins. This happens even if the Corsair dies.
The Corsair does not work in the official online app, or similar online spaces. As the Treasure must be hidden in the physical space of the game, these online spaces cannot support the Corsair.
howToRun: While preparing the first night, when all players close their eyes, put the Treasure somewhere.
On the Corsair’s first night, wake the Corsair. Give them some form of clue about where the Treasure is. Put the Corsair to sleep.
If, at any point, you see that the Demon has the Treasure, declare that the game ends and evil wins.

As the Storyteller, you should make it very hard to find the Treasure. If it only takes a minute to find, it will be too easy to just guess and win, and the rest of the game doesn't matter. Find a very well-hidden spot that every player can access equally.
Optional rule: The Treasure can be anything the Storyteller wishes. If you don’t use a character token, declare what the Treasure is for this game.
examples: Evan is the Corsair. On the first night, the Storyteller hides the Treasure under his sweater, on the floor in the back of the room. The Storyteller wakes Evan and points downwards, so that he knows to look downwards for the Treasure.
Will is the Corsair, and Aaron is the Imp. Aaron finds the Treasure on top of a picture frame on the wall. He shows it to the Storyteller. Evil wins.
tips: Find the Treasure at all costs! If that blasted Demon finds it, evil wins, and if their scurvy Minions find it, they can just give it to the Demon.
Keep yer clue secret! If the evil team doesn’t know the clues, ye’ll have better odds o’ beatin’ them to the Treasure!
Share your clue! The evil players outnumber ye, so it might be worth recruiting a crew of scallywags to help ye find the Treasure.
Keep yer eyes peeled fer players searching fer the Treasure! Be that a good player helping ye, or an evil player tryin’ to win?
Trust whoe’er finds yer Treasure! If they were the Demon, ye’d lose, and if they were a Minion, they’d give it to the Demon. If someone else finds it, ye can trust them.
characters.quote: Avast, It be the Isle of Ravenswood! We made it, laddies! Prepare yer shovels an’ get diggin’!

characters.name: Coffeeman
characters.ability: When you learn that you died, tonight, evil players' abilities work twice until dusk.
characters.creator: Squ4ll
characters.lede: The Coffeeman makes evil players hyperactive.
summaryBullets: The night after the Coffeeman learns that they died, all evil players’ abilities work twice until dusk, as if they had been affected by the Barista.
All evil players are affected by the Coffeeman, even evil Townsfolk and Travellers.
If the Coffeeman dies by execution, their ability takes effect tonight.
If the Coffeeman dies at night, their ability takes effect the night after the Storyteller announces their death.
If the Coffeeman is resurrected while the evil players are caffeinated, the evil players’ abilities no longer work twice.
In the strange case that an evil Coffeeman learns that they died, their own ability does not work twice, preventing utter chaos from being unleashed on the world.
howToRun: When the Coffeeman player is declared dead, put the [[CAFFEINATED TONIGHT]] reminder in the centre of the Grimoire.

If the [[CAFFEINATED TONIGHT]] reminder is in the Grimoire, wake each evil player. Show them the [[THIS CHARACTER SELECTED YOU]] info token, then the Coffeeman token. Evil players act twice at the appropriate time. (If the ability is optional, they may use it twice. If it is mandatory, they must use it twice.)
examples: Julia is the Coffeeman. She is executed and dies. That night, the Poisoner chooses two players to poison, and the Imp chooses two players to kill.

Claire is the Coffeeman. The Assassin kills her at night. At dawn, she learns that she died. That night, the Shabaloth chooses 4 players to kill and 2 players are regurgitated. The Assassin gains another use of their ability until dusk.
tips: Don’t die, ever! If you die, the evil team gets significantly stronger for a night. Keep yourself alive by any means necessary.
Die at the best possible time! If you think the evil team acting twice wouldn’t be very helpful tonight, today might be a good time to get yourself executed.
Find someone to protect you! An Innkeeper or Monk would happily protect you from death.

characters.name: Bladesinger
characters.ability: Once per game, at night, choose a player: they are poisoned. Once per game, at night*, choose a character: they die.
characters.creator: Squ4ll
characters.lede: The Bladesinger poisons a player and kills a character.
summaryBullets: Once per game, at night, the Bladesinger can choose a player. That player is poisoned. If the Bladesinger dies or otherwise loses their ability, that player becomes healthy.
Also once per game, at night, the Bladesinger can choose a character. That character dies.
The Bladesinger chooses a character to die, not a player. The Storyteller might need to remind players of this.
If there are multiple copies of a particular character in play and the Bladesinger chooses that character to die, only one of those characters dies. If the character is not in play, no one dies,
The Bladesinger may choose a player to poison and a character to kill on the same night. The Bladesinger chooses who to poison before they choose who to kill.
howToRun: Each night, if the Bladesinger is not marked with the [[SORCERY]] reminder, wake the Bladesinger. They either shake their head no or point to any player.

Each night except the first, if the Bladesinger is not marked with the [[SWORD]] reminder, wake the Bladesinger, if you have not already. The Bladesinger will either shake their head no or point to a character icon on the character sheet.
Put the Bladesinger to sleep.
If the Bladesinger chose a player, that player is [[poisoned]] — mark them with the [[POISONED]] reminder. Mark the Bladesinger with the [[SORCERY]] icon.
If the Bladesinger chose a character, and that character is in play, that character [[dies]] — mark them with a shroud and the [[DEAD]] reminder. Mark the Bladesinger with the [[SWORD]] icon.
examples: Zoe is the Bladesinger. On the first night, she chooses Will, who is the Chef. Will is now poisoned. On the second night, Zoe chooses Chef. Will dies.
It is the fourth night. Andrew is the Bladesinger, and has not chosen anyone yet. He chooses Sasha and then chooses Washerwoman. Sasha is poisoned, and the Washerwoman dies.
It is the second night. Joshua is the Bladesinger. He chooses Fortune Teller. Since there is no Fortune Teller in play, no one dies.
tips: Learn player’s characters as quickly as possible! The better you know players’ characters, the easier it is to kill the player you want.
Try to kill a character you’re worried about! Even if you don’t know whether or not there’s a Sage or Slayer in play, you can choose those characters to ensure they won’t cause you problems later on.

Try to combine your abilities poison and kill! Poisoning a Fool or a Sailor lets you dispose of them with ease. Poisoning a suspected Tea Lady and killing the character claimed by their neighbor lets you kill a player you normally wouldn’t be able to reach.

Poison a player you think is trying to bait you, then kill that character! If you think a player might be the Ravenkeeper, and you’re feeling risky, you can poison the player, then kill the Ravenkeeper to mess with their information.
characters.quote: With the celerity of the Great Wyrm, I dispel thy magick. With the elegance of the Iktomi, I strike thee down.

characters.name: Llongjawn
characters.ability: Each night*, choose a player: they die. If you publicly guess which player has the Treasure (once), evil wins. [+the Corsair]
characters.creator: Squ4ll
characters.lede: The Llongjawn wants the treasure for themselves.
summaryBullets: The Corsair can be in play without the Llongjawn. During the setup phase, if the Llongjawn is in play and the Corsair isn’t, the Corsair is added, and Treasure is hidden. If a Corsair is already in play, the Llongjawn doesn’t add a second Corsair.
If the Llongjawn correctly guesses which player has the Treasure on their person, and does so publicly (so that all players know they are the Demon), evil wins.
The Llongjawn must guess a player. If no player has the Treasure, the Llongjawn cannot win by guessing a player.

The Corsair (and therefore the Llongjawn) does not work in the official online app, or similar online spaces. As the Treasure must be hidden in the physical space of the game, these online spaces cannot support the Corsair.
howToRun: Each night except the first, the Llongjawn points at any player. That player [[dies]] - mark them with the [[DEAD]] reminder. Put the Llongjawn to sleep.

At any time during the game, if the Llongjawn publicly guesses which player has the treasure and is incorrect, mark the Llongjawn with the [[GUESS USED]] reminder. Future guesses by the Llongjawn player have no effect.
At any time during the game, if the Llongjawn publicly guesses which player has the treasure and is correct, the game ends. Declare that the evil team wins.
tips: Find the Treasure at all costs! If ye find the Treasure, ye win the game. Get yer crew, and get hunting!
Let the Corsair do all the work! If the Corsair has shared their clue with the lot o’ ye and narrowed down the search, ye can come in an’ take the Treasure fer yeself!
characters.quote: Damnation seize my soul if I give you quarters, or take any from you.

characters.name: Needlestick
characters.ability: Each night*, choose a player: they die. 1 player is poisoned (you know who). Their good nominator or nominee is poisoned instead.
characters.creator: Squ4ll
characters.lede: The Needlestick spreads poison through nominations.
summaryBullets: One player is poisoned. If the Needlestick dies or otherwise loses their ability, then that player becomes healthy. This player can be good or evil.
If a good player nominates the poisoned player, or the poisoned player nominates a good player, the good nominator or nominee becomes poisoned & the previously poisoned player becomes healthy.
The Needlestick always knows who is poisoned by its ability.
howToRun: While preparing the first night, one player becomes poisoned - mark them with the [[POISONED]] reminder.

If the player poisoned by the Needlestick nominates a good player, or if a good player nominates the player poisoned by the Needlestick, that player becomes poisoned, and the old player becomes healthy - move the [[POISONED]] reminder immediately if needed.
Each night, if it is the first night, or if the [[POISONED]] reminder token changed players today, wake the Needlestick. Point to the player marked [[POISONED]]. Put the Needlestick to sleep.
Each night except the first, wake the Needlestick. They point at any player. That player [[dies]] - mark them with the [[DEAD]] reminder. Put the Needlestick to sleep.
tips: Get players nominating! The more the poison moves, the harder it is to figure out who is poisoned.
Make evil players nominate! The poison can't spread to evil players, so a trusted evil player can confuse where the poison is greatly.
Encourage the poisoned player to not nominate! If the player gets information every night, like the Empath, you may want to keep the poison on them to mess with more of their info.
characters.quote: Let he who hath virtue tend to the roses, lest thorns grow in the garden of sin.

characters.name: Metyore
characters.ability: All know Townsfolk abilities work twice. The 1st player about to die may visit the Storyteller & learn the Demon. After day 1, evil wins.
characters.creator: Squ4ll
characters.lede: The Metyore brings the world to an end.
summaryBullets: At the start of the game, at dusk, all players learn that the Metyore is in play.

All Townsfolk abilities act twice. They do so at the normal time. If they would normally wake at night, they act, go to sleep, then wake to act again. If they have already used a “once per game” ability, they may use that ability again. If they have a “once per game” ability but have not used it yet, they may use it twice before dusk.
The first player "about to die" is the player who has enough votes to be executed and more votes than any other player today.
The first player about to die may visit the Storyteller and is told which player is the Demon.
After the first day, evil wins.
howToRun: Immediately after dusk on the first night, declare that the Metyore is in play. All Townsfolk abilities work twice. A player who acts twice does so at the appropriate time. (If the ability is optional, they may use it twice. If it is mandatory, they must use it twice.)
The 1st time a player receives enough votes to be executed & is "about to die", they may have a private conversation with you. Privately tell the player which player is the Demon.
If a day ends, declare that the game is over and evil wins.
examples: Adam is the Metytore. At dusk, all players learn that the Metyore is in play. Sami is the Seamstress. He uses his ability, then goes to sleep. He wakes up again, uses his ability again, then goes to sleep.
Adam is the Metyore. Sami is the Seamstress. Sami is nominated and is about to die. He visits the Storyteller and learns that Adam is the Demon. Adam is executed, and good wins.
characters.quote: Yes, it's you I welcome death with. As the world caves in.

characters.name: Catch
characters.ability: Once per game, a player might die.
characters.creator: Squ4ll
summaryBullets: The Storyteller may kill one player during the game.
The death can happen during the day or during the night.
The Catch can kill any player, good or evil.
It is not known whether a particular death was due to the Catch or to another ability.
Use the Catch if players are stalling the game, or to make it harder to tell why a player died.
howToRun: When you see it fit, you may kill any player. That player [[dies]]. Mark them with a shroud. If they die during the day, declare to all players that this player dies.
examples: There are 4 players alive. It is the 9th day. No one is being executed, and the Imp is choosing dead players. The Storyteller declares that Alex dies.
It is the second night. The Storyteller kills Zach. At dawn, Zach learns that he died.
It is the first day. Evan nominates and dies. The Storyteller used the Catch to make Evan believe he was killed by the Witch.
